title: centaurea nallihanensis
intro: Centaurea nallihanensis, also known as Nallihan knapweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae), native to Turkey. Traditionally, Centaurea nallihanensis has been used in folk medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone content. The plant is believed to possess various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Centaurea nallihanensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
